title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 24th April 2026 || TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Signs some buying may come if lower levels closer to 1200 arrive; margin guidance held at 22%.
recommendations.detail: Watch how Infosys trades after results and whether it stabilises at lower levels and rises. The 21% year-on-year jump is basically currency gains - on a constant-currency basis it grew only 1.5 to 3%. The one positive is that the operating margin guidance was maintained at 22%. If levels closer to 1200 come, there are signs some buying may come.

recommendations.note: Corporate official changes - new CEO appointed; activity possible again.
recommendations.detail: Dabur is one where there could be some activity again because of corporate official changes - a CEO has been appointed. Whether any further new changes come has to be watched.

recommendations.note: New facility opening; slowly gaining strength.
recommendations.detail: Azad Engineering could be active with a new facility opening. He said Azad is slowly gaining strength, and noted this was also mentioned in the recent workshop. The anchor later compared it to Sansera as a high-quality precision engineering company.

recommendations.note: Adani group results better than expected broadly; signs Adani Energy could be active today.
recommendations.detail: In the Adani group, results are coming in better than expected for many companies, and there are signs Adani Energy could be somewhat active today. Note that Adani Energy's own numbers were among the disappointing ones and the stock opened negative on the day.

recommendations.note: Very good number with 43% growth; a break above 350 signals a decent run.
recommendations.detail: Tata Capital's result is definitely a very good number - 43% growth came. The stock stayed subdued after the IPO but the range has been good between 300 and 350. If a breakout comes and it crosses 350, there are signs of a decent run in Tata Capital. He said to observe it.

recommendations.note: TMT bar prices near Rs 60,000/tonne; this and next quarter's results look good.
recommendations.detail: There is quite a bit of buzz in steel stocks, which became a headline in the Mint paper. Prices have risen a lot - TMT bars are at almost Rs 60,000 a tonne now. This quarter and the next quarter results look like they will be good, helped by pre-monsoon construction. He said to buy the other good TMT companies; the anchor added the caveat to stick to good, strong companies.

recommendations.note: Already run to ~1,050 but still good potential; a risky dark-horse bet.
recommendations.detail: Mentioned by him earlier, Steel Exchange has already gone to about 1,050, but there is still good potential in it as part of the steel/TMT price theme. The anchor immediately cautioned that names like Steel Exchange are slightly risky bets and have to be taken as dark horses rather than as core holdings.

recommendations.note: Can be held; results due that day.
recommendations.detail: A viewer (Neeraja) who bought Adani Green Energy at Rs 1,142 last year asked whether to hold or exit, with a result due that day. His answer: this can be held - if it's a yes or no, then he has said hold.

recommendations.note: A bit weak; sell on rallies, no surprises expected.
recommendations.detail: A viewer (Teja from Guntur) holding Bata India from Rs 1,900 asked what to do. Bata is a bit weak, so sell on rallies is what he would say, because he does not think surprises are going to come in Bata.
